question:
Will Elon Musk post 300-319 tweets from November 7 to November 14, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from November 7 12:00 PM ET to November 14, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

evidences.title: Twitter under Elon Musk - Wikipedia
evidences.content: A computational analysis published on November 1, 2024, found potential algorithmic bias on X in favour of Republican Party "Republican Party (United States)") supporters. The study, which looked at more than 56,000 posts, identified a system-wide "structural break" on the platform around July 13, 2024, a date which coincides with Elon Musk's endorsement of Donald Trump in the 2024 US presidential election. From that point on, Elon Musk and other Republican commentators saw increased visibility for their posts. According to the researchers behind the study, this "could imply an algorithmic adjustment". On January 17, 2025, the European Commission requested to see X's internal documentation about its algorithms "and any recent changes made to it", following accusations of manipulation [...] 66. ↑ MCDONALD, ANDREW; WEBBER, ESTHER (August 7, 2024). evidences.title: X's audience shifts right without losing reach
evidences.content: X has seen a shift to the right in its audience, without losing overall reach, according to our Digital News Report 2025. Here are three things to know about this issue: 1️⃣ Usage of X for news is stable or increasing across many markets. The biggest uplift is in the United States (+8 points for news from last year to 23%), Australia and Poland ( both +6). 2️⃣ Since Elon Musk bought the social network in 2022, many more right-leaning people, notably young men, have flocked to it, while some progressive audiences have left or are using it less frequently. Twitter pre-2022 was populated mostly by those on the progressive side of the political spectrum, but the proportion that self-identifies on the right has grown after Elon Musk took over. 3️⃣ In the US, last year’s presidential election [...] ⸻ 👀 If you post content (or run a business): This is your heads-up: reach will no longer come from tricks like hashtags or engagement bait. You’ll need to write in a way the AI can understand and rank. For all figures except those that explicitly require timestamps and are sensitive to exact hour/time, I convert these timestamps to CST. That corresponds to the timezone in Texas, where Elon resides and spends the majority of his time. Ultimately, I was able to gather data from 7/27/24 to 9/12/24 (48 days). #### 2 thoughts on “Elon Musk definitely tweets too much” 1. Image 10 ArcosJanuary 7, 2025 at 6:13 pm Reply Nice work! From 7/27/24 to 9/12/24, he hasaveraged nearly 68 tweets per day. I estimate that hespends ~2.7 hours/day on Twitterusing conservative assumptions. evidences.title: How Traders Are Making Thousands Counting Elon Musk's ...
evidences.content: There's a market on Poly Market right now with $23 million in volume, $15 million traded in the last 24 hours. And it's not about war. It's not about an election. It's not even about crypto. It's about how many times Elon Musk is tweeting this week. 30 different brackets, 0 to 19 tweets, 20 to 39 all the way up to 580 plus. Thousands of people spending real money trying to predict the posting habits of one man. And somehow this might be the smartest market on the entire platform. Let me explain. So here's how it works. Every week, Poly Market opens a new market. How many times will Elon Musk post on X from Monday to Monday? Posts, quote tweets, and reposts all count. Replies do not, unless they show up on his main feed. The market splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. [...] splits into 30 brackets, each covering a 20 tweet range. You buy the bracket you think he lands in, and if you're right, your share pays out a dollar. If you're wrong, they go to zero. Right now, the current week, February 24th to March 3rd, the market is pricing 300 to 319 tweets as the most likely outcome at 24%. The bracket below it, 280 to 299 is 22% and 320 to 339 is also 22%. So the m...

evidences.title: X under Musk's leadership: Substantial hate and no reduction in ...
evidences.content: controlling for overall repost popularity. For any account that posts at least ten reposts, we record the ID of all reposts then create a TF-IDF vector by calculating each repost ID’s “term frequency” (how often it appears for each account) multiplied by their inverse document frequency (how many accounts share this repost). Unlike, e.g., simple repost frequency, TF-IDF gives greater weight to reposts that appear unusually often among a few accounts and less weight to reposts that are popular and broadly shared across X. Accounts whose vectors have a cosine similarity in the top 0.1 percentile are considered coordinated. Finally, activity similarity is defined as accounts posting (reposting or writing original posts, replies, or quote posts) nearly simultaneously. Following previous work [...] We find no significant change in coordinated account activity or engagement. That said, we see an increase in the number of active bot-like accounts, and a substantial upward trend in cryptocurrency posts, which often contain spam. All of these results suggest no decrease, and a potential increase, in activity among inauthentic accounts. This could have grave consequences not merely for us...

Topics Social Media X/Twitter Elon Musk [...] The most recent big change to the platform occurred just last month when Musk made most "likes" on the platform private. Previously, users could click the "like count" and see a list of users who liked a post. Now, only the user who published the post could see which users liked it. Third-parties can no longer see who liked what. Now, with Musk confirming the removal of repost and like buttons from posts, he will institute another change he's long wanted to make. "Next, we'll remove all the action buttons with their superfluous interaction counts from the main timeline," Musk posted") in a subscriber-only post on X in October of last year. "Just view count will show, unless you tap into a post." [...] It's worth noting that during that same month, Musk also had X developers remove headlines from article links shared within posts because he didn't like how it looked aesthetically. Although some LinkedIn posts get reposted more often than X, the median is pretty close. Bluesky trails a bit behind, but nowhere near as far as the follower count would suggest. But #3 is a long way from the first two (google and direct). LinkedIn may be #3, but is the source for only 3.3% of the site's traffic.1 1: I'm not sure how much traffic from these networks does not get properly flagged as a source in the analytics, so I'm wary of concluding too much from this figure. evidences.title: How Did I Export And Analyze Elon Musk’s Tweets To Find...
evidences.content: After exporting and analyzing Elon Musk’s tweets, the biggest takeaway is how social media data can reveal direct market influences that might otherwise be hidden. By aligning tweet timestamps with real-world events and price movements, you can spot patterns—like immediate surges in Dogecoin’s value or shifts in Tesla’s stock—that traditional research often misses. The ability to filter, archive, and systematically study tweets transforms Twitter from a noisy feed into an actionable research tool for investors, marketers, and researchers alike. With API-compliant tools like Circleboom Twitter, the process becomes efficient, accurate, and safe, allowing anyone to dive deep into public sentiment and engagement metrics within minutes. [...] That’s when I discovered a systematic way to export and analyze tweets in bulk — turning an endless stream of posts into actionable data. By leveraging tools like Circleboom, I quickly gathered Elon Musk’s public tweets and cross-referenced them with market data to reveal fascinating connections that most people simply overlook. In this blog post, I'll walk you through my entire process, share surprising findings, and explain how you too can uti...

evidences.title: Elon Musk’s Tesla finally sets a shareholder meeting date amid doubts about his long-denied $56 billion pay package | Fortune
evidences.content: # Trendingnow # 1 # 2 # 3 # 1 # 2 # 3 # Elon Musk’s Tesla finally sets a shareholder meeting date amid doubts about his long-denied $56 billion pay package Amanda Gerut Amanda Gerut Elon Musk wearing black in the Oval Office in May, 2025. Better late than never. Tesla announced on Thursday it will hold an annual shareholders meeting on Nov. 6. The announcement comes some 24 hours after a letter from more than two dozen investors in the $952 billion electric-vehicle maker pressed the board on when it would next go mano a mano with its shareholders. [...] The last annual shareholders meeting was a high-stakes event that saw investors approve CEO Elon Musk’s controversial pay package a second time and green-light a move from Delaware to Texas. Under Texas law, Tesla is required to hold an annual meeting within 13 months of the last one, which was held on June 13, 2024. Tesla notified investors about the meeting and set a July 31 deadline for shareholders to submit proposals. Harvard professor of law and economics John Coates told Fortune Texas law provides that companies like Tesla are to hold annual meetings, and that any shareholder can demand one in court if one has not been hel...
